text:
An Australian athlete has become the latest victim to thugs who smashed in the windows of his van and stole his personal items - but one device helped them locate some important gear.

An Australian athlete has had personal items stolen from his van in another alarming incident that is overshadowing the Paris Olympic Games.

BMX star Logan Martin took to Instagram on Wednesday night to share vision of the black vehicle with the driver and passenger windows smashed in.

"So our van was broken into last night. Lucky my bikes weren't in there. My bike bags were there with some stuff in there," he said to his 510,000 followers.

"Crazy start to the trip."

It is understood the robbery happened in Belgium, which borders France.

A short second video showed police officers inspecting the car.

Martin later found most of his personal items, such as gloves, helmets and a massage chair, dumped and scattered in what appeared to be a park.

"They ate your choccies (chocolates)," the woman filming joked.

The group located the missing luggage by air tags which is tracked by the phone.

"Our van got broken into overnight. Luckily most of our stuff was in our room and we managed to recover a lot of it too," the 30-year-old wrote in his post.

"I lost my wallet, backpack and a few other things but overall it didn’t end up being too bad. It’s unfortunate when people feel the need to do things like this."

The robbery continues to shine a light on the dangers Australian athletes and tourists are facing in Europe, just two days out from the opening ceremony.

Two Channel 9 crew were ambushed by thieves as they were walking back to their accommodation in the outskirts of Paris on Monday afternoon.

They alleged thugs tried to steal one of their backpacks and were assaulted.

The incident was reported to police.

On Sunday, an Australian woman visiting Paris claimed she was gang raped by five men.

Vision emerged of the 25-year-old woman seeking help from bystanders at a kebab shop Boulevard de Clichy near the Moulin Rouge in Paris' 18th arrondissement.

Other customers tried to comfort the woman who was in tears with the dress she was wearing "backwards" and partially torn off, witnesses claimed.

The clip, obtained by 7News, also showed the Australian tourist signalling to a kebab shop owner that a man who walked in to the shop was one of her alleged attackers.

The staff member and the man got into a tussle before the latter fled.

Police later arrived at the scene and took statements, with the Australian woman telling officers she was raped by five men of African appearance.

Paris prosecutor's office said it is investigating the charge of gang rape "likely to have been committed on the night of July 19 to 20, 2024".

Australian competitors have been warned to take extra care when they are outside the athletes village and to not wear their official green and gold team uniform.

Police have been deployed across France and in other neighbouring countries to ensure safety for tens of thousands of spectators and athletes flying in.

But the French force has reportedly been stretched, with authorities focused on security at the Seine River ahead of the opening ceremony on Friday (local time).

title: Australian BMX star Logan Martin loses some personal items after thieves broke into van in Belgium ahead of Paris Olympics | Sky News Australia
description: An Australian athlete has had personal items stolen from his van in another alarming incident that is overshadowing the Paris Olympic Games.
